summary: Equity is the backbone of long-term wealth creation — but should you invest directly in stocks, or through mutual funds? 🤔 In this episode of This or That, Bhartendra Singh speaks with Sneha Jain, CEO of Wealth Trust, to simplify equity investing, bust myths around SIPs, and give you a clear roadmap for building wealth.

chapters.label: Intro & Highlights — Why equity feels invisible compared to real estate & gold
Equity 101 — What you’re really buying when you invest in stocks
How to start with direct stocks (large-caps first)
Evaluating a company: promoters, debt, ROE/ROCE, PE
IPOs for beginners? Why incumbents are safer
Thinking like an owner — cycles vs company quality
Top-down analysis: macros, rates, geopolitics
Bottom-up analysis: sectors and stock shortlist
Consideration to evaluate a stock
How many stocks is enough?
Futures & Options — why most should avoid & trading thrill
Using curated RA portfolios (Smallcase, Pickright, Starfolio), PMS & AIF
Portfolio mix idea: 60–65% via mutual funds
SIP is not the investment — know the underlying
Mutual fund basics & Constructing a Portfolio : Large, Mid, Small explained
Large-cap choice: Index vs Active
Large & Mid-cap blend — where alpha comes from
Small-cap: high alpha, high anxiety
Flexicap vs Multicap — what’s the difference?
Core–Satellite approach with thematic/global funds
Building a Mutual Fund Portfolio - Avoid mistakes & decluttering
Small-cap surge & recency bias trap
How to pick funds: turnover, consistency, fit
A simple starter MF plan
Lumpsum? Hybrid and multi-asset options
Final blueprint for equity investing

tool.label: Asset Allocator
tool.reason: This episode is really about your mix. Put in your numbers and see the equity, debt, gold and international split that actually fits you.

disclaimer: This podcast is for educational purposes only. It should not be considered financial advice. Please consult a SEBI-registered investment advisor before making any investment decisions.

seo.title: Stocks vs Mutual Funds: The Truth About SIP & Equity Investing | Sneha Jain
